issue_title: Default processors for certain django model fields
issue_body:
This follows from pr #157.

I plan on using this helper along with the `make_boolean_checkmark` helper a lot. Basically show all boolean fields as checkmarks and all (ForeignKey'ed) related models as links. So every datatable created will most probably have several of these combined. 

Therefore, it would be very convenient, if I could assign default processors for these field types (i.e., BooleanField, and ForeignKey). Such as:

    boolean_field_processor = ..
    foreign_key_processor = ..

And then specific processors defined as usual (which override the defaults if coincided):

    processors = [ ...

Default processors would probably be put in a parent class,  thus making configuration much easier. I used these two types, but there could be others as well.

This release unblocks Pants work which upgrades to subprocess32.

issue_title: Slice ScroogeGen out to contrib
issue_body:
ScroogeGen is currently a core plugin but its only used by Twitter.  As discussed at the January 2015 summit, this is a good candidate to be sliced out to contrib and get its own pypi sdist.  Do this and update the release script to include contrib/ sdists.

The contrib/ layout was finalized here: https://groups.google.com/d/topic/pants-devel/9EREtXy5lR4/discussion
Long story short - @patricklaw's initial proposal wins, ie for a pure python plugin:
+ contrib/[plugin]/src/python/pants-contrib/[rest]
+ contrib/[plugin]/tests/python/pants-contrib-test/[rest]

issue_title: Fix future::Shared and switch back to single-threaded scheduling
issue_body:
To work around https://github.com/alexcrichton/futures-rs/issues/330, we've switched to launching all tasks on a CpuPool. The added overhead of lock contention and context switching into the pool for all Nodes slows things down by about 20%, so we should remove usage of the pool in `graph::Entry::new` once that issue is fixed.

It's possible that #4297 will improve the situation here, because there will be fewer, larger tasks.

issue_title: The leveled predicate change to build graph introduced some overtraversals
issue_body: We have a target with many overlapping edges that experiences superlinear slowdowns over the previous behavior. I'm working on a fix that maintains the existing observable behavior while reducing the number of unnecessary traversals.

issue_title: Reinstate the clean of vt.results_dir in NodeResolve
issue_body:
The NodeResolve task used to clean the vt.results_dir but that was recently removed in 85174866852aa1387cb34a7cdfd1f6642b6480c4.

The argument was that `safe_mkdir(results_dir, clean=True)` was redundant and removing it stopped exceptions like `Exception message: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted: '/Users/yic/workspace/source/.pants.d/resolve/node/252d64521cf9/<target.id>/current'`.

I think that this was a misdiagnosis, and that the clean should be reinstated. The purpose of that call wasn't the mkdir, as much as the clean. The exceptions are below proposed as a consequence of the clean logic.

**Motivation for reinstating the clean**
The `clean` is actually useful if there has been a ctrl-c or error during a resolve - the directory could hold a halfway state and best be blown away. With a bad resolve, it is possibly vital to clean.

**Proposed cause of the exceptions**
The exceptions are from the times when the above scenario has occurred. For one reason or another, the vt is invalid and the `results_dir` had something in it.

In this case, the `safe_mkdir` call would correctly clean the directory and remake it. But the file pointer being passed by the SimpleCodegen superclass is the `current` results_dir - which is expected to be a symlink. When `safe_mkdir` recreated the directory, it converted it from a symlink into a real dir, so the next run will raise an Exception when the cache_manager unsuccessfully attempts to unlink `current`. 

**Disclaimer**
Does bad state get cleaned somewhere else along the pipeline? Not that I see with the below repro

**Repro POC**
On pants master, drop a trace on in the invalidation block:
```
@@ -84,6 +84,7 @@ class NodeResolve(NodeTask):
         for vt in invalidation_check.all_vts:
           target = vt.target
           if not vt.valid:
+            import pdb; pdb.set_trace()
```

Repro is to touch a file under the  `vt.results_dir` and clean with safe_mkdir. Then ctrl-C and re-run the resolve to see exception. Here is mine:
```
mateo:pants mateo$ ./pants resolve contrib/node:
<snip>
16:02:30 00:02     [node]
                   Invalidated 5 targets.> /Users/mateo/dev/pants/contrib/node/src/python/pants/contrib/node/tasks/node_resolve.py(88)execute()
-> resolver_for_target_type = self._resolver_for_target(target).global_instance()
(Pdb) from pants.util.dirutil import safe_mkdir
(Pdb) import os
(Pdb) pp vt.results_dir
u'/Users/mateo/dev/pants/.pants.d/resolve/node/252d64521cf9/contrib.node.examples.src.node.preinstalled-project.preinstalled-project/current'
(Pdb) os.listdir(vt.results_dir)
[u'mateo.txt']
(Pdb) safe_mkdir(vt.results_dir, clean=True)
(Pdb) os.listdir(vt.results_dir)
[]
(Pdb)
16:02:30 00:02       [install]
16:04:08 01:40   [complete]
               ABORTED
Interrupted by user.
mateo:pants mateo$ ./pants resolve contrib/node::
<snip>
Exception message: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted: '/Users/mateo/dev/pants/.pants.d/resolve/node/252d64521cf9/contrib.node.examples.src.node.preinstalled-project.preinstalled-project/current'

mateo:pants mateo$
```
**Robust solutions**
The ideal fix would probably be restore the clean and do one or both of:
1) Ensure tasks pass around the stable file path for `results_dir` instead of the `current` symlink
2) Give safe_mkdir an understanding of symlinks, so that it cleans the dereferenced directory.*

NB: (2) is a bit sticky, since it raises the philosophical question of whether that allows deleting directories outside the buildroot.

`safe_mkdir` will happily create directories outside the buildroot, although I do not believe it exercises that power. But creating a directory is not as scary as recursively deleting. My instinct would be to add a check to make sure that the path is under the `pants_workdir` if you went that route.

**Quick Solutions**
The first two are not ideal since they only solve this situation for NodeResolve and leave other tasks to fend for themselves, but easy to patch in quickly.

One of:
1.
```
           if not vt.valid:
+            safe_mkdir(os.path.realpath(vt.results_dir), clean=True)
             resolver_for_target_type = self._resolver_for_target(target).global_instance()
             resolver_for_target_type.resolve_target(self, target, vt.results_dir, node_paths)
```
2. Manually do the delete with shutil.rmtree or something
3. Perhaps change `safe_mkdir(fp, clean=True)` to only delete the contents instead of deleting the entire directory and recreating. Possibly useful in other ways, since silently recreating means that it could also silently change the directory owner or permissions.

issue_title: Move "cross-platform" tagged tests to final two shards
issue_body:
As we've introduced more platform dependent code (and as we land #5815 in particular), we've gained some risk that some of pants' unit/integration tests are not being run on OSX in places where it might be meaningful. Before landing #5815, we should do something to ensure that tests (for which it might matter) run on both OSX and Linux.

I'd propose that we use target tags to do this (for now) by adding a tag to test targets that might have different behaviour on different platforms. We'd then run all of the tagged targets on an OSX shard: most likely we should reuse/rename the OSX rust tests shard for this (to avoid the overhead of adding another shard that would need to bootstrap toolchains).

issue_title: JVM subsystem options are invisible in task help
issue_body:
In pants 0.0.39, some changes to the option subsystem have landed that enable some cool things, like using options in the JVM subsystem anywhere (cool!). However, these options are invisible in the help, unless `--help-advanced` is specified. There are two issues with this -- `--help-advanced` contains a ton of options now, so it's hard to find things. And secondly, I don't want to tell users to use `--help-advanced` -- the whole point of that is to hide things from the general populace, that are only necessary for pants admins.

For example, when trying to enable debugging for junit tests. Compare this:

```
[prometheus source2 (master)]$ ./pants test -h |grep debug
```

To this:

```
[prometheus source2 (master)]$ ./pants test -h --help-advanced|grep debug
--[no-]jvm-test-junit-debug (default: None)
    Run the JVM with remote debugging.
--jvm-test-junit-debug-port=<int> (default: 5005)
    The JVM will listen for a debugger on this port.
--jvm-test-junit-debug-args="['str1','str2',...]" (default: ['-Xdebug','-Xrunjdwp:transport=dt_socket,server=y,suspend=y,address={debug_port}'])
    The JVM remote-debugging arguments. {debug_port} will be replaced with the
    value of the --debug-port option.
```

I think either these options need to be promoted to the task level, or there needs to be some way to figure out what options the JVM sub-system supports, i.e.:

```
[prometheus source2 (master)]$ ./pants jvm -h

Unknown goal: jvm
```

This is discussed further here:

https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pants-devel/fPyKDLnGOKI

issue_title: The gopkg.in fetcher handles v0 incorrectly
issue_body:
See here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/contrib/go/src/python/pants/contrib/go/subsystems/fetchers.py#L464-L466

The code maps v0 to master unconditionally, but instead should fall back to master - only for v0 - if there is no matching tag. See 'Version Zero' docs here: http://labix.org/gopkg.in

An example case is here: http://gopkg.in/fsnotify.v0
As of today that should resolve to v0.9.3 - a tag

issue_title: SourceRoot.here() does not work at the build root
issue_body: The rel_path (relative path from the build root), gets registered as '.' which causes all Target.target_base to be computed as '.' (ie: "here" relative to the target's spec path).

issue_title: ./rbt shouldn't use origin master as its point to diff against
issue_body:
If you follow the contrib guide, pants/pants will be named `upstream`, but rbt uses origin master. For new contributors that can make things confusing because commonly you'll pull upstream master and then branch for a feature branch.

Could we fix that so rbt will always use pants/pants as the source of record?

issue_title: Assuming PGDATA wrong when --restore-spec is used
issue_body:
I created a new Cluster only to do a restore.

The PGDATA(Let's call PGDATA1) original is ``/var/lib/pgsql/9.4/data`` and the restore PGDATA(Let's call PGDATA2) is ``/storage/test_restore/data``.

And I created a JSON to tablespaces lynks.

````{
    "16393": {
        "loc": "/storage/test_restore/data/TBS_DATA",
        "link": "pg_tblspc/16393"
    },
    "16394": {
        "loc": "/storage/test_restore/data/TBS_INDEXES",
        "link": "pg_tblspc/16394"
    },
    "tablespaces": [
        "16393","16394"
    ]
}````

issue_title: Add `shell=True` to `subprocess.Popen` calls (Pyinstaller)
issue_body:
I can confirm that imageio+ffmpeg works with Pyinstaller on Windows and Anaconda Python 2.7 (#32).
However, when using the [ffmpeg](https://github.com/imageio/imageio/blob/master/imageio/plugins/ffmpeg.py) plugin in a frozen application on Windows, and when seeking through an avi video (`imageio.get_reader`) using `get_data`, an empty command Window pops up with the title ("path/to/ffmpeg-win32-v3.2.4.exe"). These command windows are not shown when the keyword argument `shell=True` is set in `sp.Popen`: https://github.com/paulmueller/imageio/commit/b5aeb617fb6b1a74ff30c40ae998bac31b526f39

If this does not break compatibility, I would recommend adding that extra keyword argument everywhere. I can create a pull request if you are too busy yourself.

As the Ubuntu package will pre-compile Python byte code upon installation, it unfortunately fails:

```
Setting up kolibri (0.7.1~b1.dev0+git.13.gf1d43ed-0ubuntu1) ...

SyntaxError: invalid syntax

    raise exception_type, self._exception, self._traceback
                        ^
SyntaxError: invalid syntax

  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/kolibri/dist/kolibri_exercise_perseus_plugin/static/mathjax/kathjax.py", line 32
    print "%s exists, exiting" % mjdir

SyntaxError: Missing parentheses in call to 'print'

The command py3compile -p kolibri -V 3.4- exited with exit code 1.
dpkg: error processing package kolibri (--configure):
 subprocess installed post-installation script returned error exit status 1
E: Sub-process /usr/bin/dpkg returned an error code (1)
```

I'll patch this up in the debian package, a temporary measure. Meanwhile, here are the tracking issues:

 * [x] https://github.com/ftao/python-ifcfg/issues/21
 * [x] https://github.com/learningequality/iceqube/issues/31
 * [x] https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri-installer-debian/issues/12

 * [x] Cherrypy wrong version installed in `kolibri/dist` -- see: https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri-installer-debian/issues/15

As for the `kathjax.py`, I think this is a build file that has entered the source wrongly. But because of all the horrible git submodules, it's really hard to figure out where the issue should be raised.

### Expected behavior

Debian package can work without patches

### User-facing consequences

Need more info

### Errors and logs

...

### Steps to reproduce

You can compile all Python code recursively using Python3 like so:

    python3 -m compileall -q kolibri/

### Context

All releases that depend on `ifcfg=>0.12`, `iceqube` or the Perseus plugin... so all releases :)

issue_title: Rename all instances of (.*)composer.yaml and composer
issue_body: The module should be referred to as (ie in logging, etc) as generate and any reference to .composer.manifest or composer.yaml needs to be .layers.manifest and layers.yaml We should aim to support composer.yaml but throw a W deprecation notice when it's used. In 2.0 we'll drop it officially. All documentation should be updated to reflect layers.yaml and generate.

issue_title: SSAARead is unhashable
issue_body: SSAARead is (ultimately) a subclass of `Read` which has a `__hash__` function that looks for `self.quality`. There's no `quality` on an `SSAARead`, so the hashing fails. Add a `__hash__` method to `SSAARead`.

On Windows there appears to be a breaking change with the release of version 222 of pypiwin32 [https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pypiwin32](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pypiwin32)

**Call stack:**

docker.errors.DockerException: Install pypiwin32 package to enable npipe:// support

**Installed Packages:**
```
$> pip list
asn1crypto (0.24.0)
certifi (2018.1.18)

**Quick pointers:**
Per [win32-requirements.txt](https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/master/win32-requirements.txt) the minimum version specified is 219. issue_title: Serial DFU pinging fails if bootloader is not ready
issue_body:
Serial DFU could fail if bootloader board is not ready when trying to ping. This could happen during a combined DFU.

In dfu_transport_serial.py in open() the bootloader board is pinged twice.
If the first __ping() fails the complete DFU operation fails. It looks like the first __ping() should be removed.

Also, when looking into the __ping() function, I see that instead of returning an error code, it will raise an exception when the bootloader board is not responding. This will break the "waiting for ping"-functionality in open().

issue_body:
[@MrElusive](https://github.com/MrElusive) and [@ihouses](https://github.com/ihouses) from #740:

> 
> This code works:
> ```
> with nidcpower.Session("4145") as session:
>     with session.initiate():
>         voltage_measurement = session['0'].measure(nidcpower.MeasurementTypes.VOLTAGE)
>         print("Measurement: {}".format(voltage_measurement))
> ```
> 
> This code doesn't (error -1074118650 (session not running) at the call to measure)
> ```
> with nidcpower.Session("4145") as session:
>     session.initiate()
>     voltage_measurement = session['0'].measure(nidcpower.MeasurementTypes.VOLTAGE)
>     print("Measurement: {}".format(voltage_measurement))
> ```

Our intention was for either to work. In the second example, client would be responsible for calling `session.abort()` explicitly or closing the session.

issue_body: There are plenty of places in the code where we do `variable.data.new().resize_(some_shape).fill_(some_value)`.  This is an annoying pattern to see in code and to remember when writing code.  We should add a method to `nn.util` that does this in a configurable way, and replace all places in the code where we do this with the new method.

issue_title: Failing admin healthmonitor tempest tests
issue_body:
The [admin health monitor tests](https://github.com/openstack/neutron-lbaas/blob/master/neutron_lbaas/tests/tempest/v2/api/test_health_monitor_admin.py) create a loadbalancer and a listener

```
    """
    Tests the following operations in the Neutron-LBaaS API using the
    REST client for Health Monitors with ADMIN role:
    create health monitor with missing tenant_id
    create health monitor with empty tenant id
    create health monitor with another tenant_id
    """

    @classmethod
    def resource_setup(cls):
        super(TestHealthMonitors, cls).resource_setup()
        if not test.is_extension_enabled('lbaas', 'network'):
            msg = "lbaas extension not enabled."
            raise cls.skipException(msg)
        network_name = data_utils.rand_name('network-')
        cls.network = cls.create_network(network_name)
        cls.subnet = cls.create_subnet(cls.network)
        cls.load_balancer = cls._create_load_balancer(
            tenant_id=cls.subnet.get('tenant_id'),
            vip_subnet_id=cls.subnet.get('id'))
        cls.listener = cls._create_listener(
            loadbalancer_id=cls.load_balancer.get('id'),
            protocol='HTTP', protocol_port=80)
        cls.pool = cls._create_pool(
            protocol='HTTP', lb_algorithm='ROUND_ROBIN',
            listener_id=cls.listener.get('id'))
```

When we go to create the loadbalancer, we created and activated a partition named ` {'name': u'6e9a4a294f834'}` (http://ci-logs.a10cloud.com//r/master-a10-neutron-lbaas-lbaasv2-dsvm-api-acos-272/1/logs/screen-q-svc.txt.gz?#_2016-03-11_02_33_34_530)

When we go to create the listener, we create and activate a partiiton named `{'name': u'a8f4c21615f84'}` (http://ci-logs.a10cloud.com//r/master-a10-neutron-lbaas-lbaasv2-dsvm-api-acos-272/1/logs/screen-q-svc.txt.gz?#_2016-03-11_02_33_37_610)

The virtual server we created for the loadbalancer doesn't exist in this partition to create the listener on.

My guess is that in this test cast the listener's `tenant_id` isn't the same as the loadbalancer's `tenant_id`.

In `A10Context` we do this:
```
    def get_tenant_id(self):
        if hasattr(self.openstack_lbaas_obj, 'tenant_id'):
            self.tenant_id = self.openstack_lbaas_obj.tenant_id
        else:
            self.tenant_id = self.openstack_lbaas_obj['tenant_id']
```
For lbaasv2 we should probably be getting the tenant_id (at least for the purpose of which partition to activate) off of the object's `root_loadbalancer`.

issue_title: deis run should escape commands
issue_body:
`heroku run 'rails r "puts RUBY_VERSION"'` performs as expected, outputs
```
2.2.1
```

`deis run 'rails r "puts RUBY_VERSION"'` hangs infinitely.
`fleetctl list-units | grep 'run\.'`:
```
example_v56.run.12.service				8059d8cd.../192.168.0.3	inactive	dead
```

`fleetctl journal example_v56.run.12.service`:
```
-- Logs begin at Tue 2015-03-24 11:30:59 UTC, end at Tue 2015-03-31 07:02:02 UTC. --
Mar 31 07:01:58 staging-coreos-3 systemd[1]: [/run/fleet/units/example_v56.run.12.service:7] Trailing garbage, ignoring.
Mar 31 07:01:58 staging-coreos-3 systemd[1]: example_v56.run.12.service lacks both ExecStart= and ExecStop= setting. Refusing.
Mar 31 07:01:59 staging-coreos-3 systemd[1]: [/run/fleet/units/example_v56.run.12.service:7] Trailing garbage, ignoring.
Mar 31 07:01:59 staging-coreos-3 systemd[1]: example_v56.run.12.service lacks both ExecStart= and ExecStop= setting. Refusing.
Mar 31 07:02:00 staging-coreos-3 systemd[1]: [/run/fleet/units/example_v56.run.12.service:7] Trailing garbage, ignoring.
Mar 31 07:02:00 staging-coreos-3 systemd[1]: example_v56.run.12.service lacks both ExecStart= and ExecStop= setting. Refusing.
Mar 31 07:02:00 staging-coreos-3 systemd[1]: [/run/fleet/units/example_v56.run.12.service:7] Trailing garbage, ignoring.
Mar 31 07:02:00 staging-coreos-3 systemd[1]: example_v56.run.12.service lacks both ExecStart= and ExecStop= setting. Refusing.
Mar 31 07:02:01 staging-coreos-3 systemd[1]: [/run/fleet/units/example_v56.run.12.service:7] Trailing garbage, ignoring.
Mar 31 07:02:01 staging-coreos-3 systemd[1]: example_v56.run.12.service lacks both ExecStart= and ExecStop= setting. Refusing.
```

`fleetctl cat example_v56.run.12.service`:

```
[Unit]
Description=example_v56.run.12 admin command

[Service]
ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "IMAGE=$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/host 2>&1):$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/port 2>&1)/example:v56; docker pull $IMAGE"
ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "docker inspect example_v56.run.12 >/dev/null 2>&1 && docker rm -f example_v56.run.12 || true"
ExecStart=/bin/sh -c "IMAGE=$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/host 2>&1):$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/port 2>&1)/example:v56; docker run --name example_v56.run.12 --entrypoint=/runner/init -a stdout -a stderr $IMAGE 'rails r "puts RUBY_VERSION"'"
TimeoutStartSec=20m
```

So the problem here is that arguments to runner are not escaped. Not only this breakes unit file, but can also be used to add extra arguments to `sh` invocation, which is a security issue. No user input should ever be interpolated unsanitized.

issue_title: store: Re-template ceph.conf after daemon/monitor are started
issue_body:
In the bin/boot scripts for [store-daemon](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/e99ae10dd9e7fa767a9a7b48d76a33ad07534555/store/daemon/bin/boot#L54), [store-metadata](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/e99ae10dd9e7fa767a9a7b48d76a33ad07534555/store/metadata/bin/boot#L64), [store-monitor](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/e99ae10dd9e7fa767a9a7b48d76a33ad07534555/store/monitor/bin/boot#L88), we template `/etc/ceph/ceph.conf` with confd, and then exec the Ceph process. Unfortunately, this means that for long-running store components, we won't pick up changes to monitors until the container is restarted.

I think that the Ceph process will be happy as it only reads the configuration to get a list of monitors to query for state, but for anyone running `ceph -s` within the container, they'll see a lot of faults. 

I'm hoping this can be solved as part of #2701 when we rewrite the bin/boot scripts in Go, as we'll need to run both the daemon process and confd.

400 BAD REQUEST
Container type run does not exist in application
```

issue_title: deis-builder doesn't honor registry protocol
issue_body:
I'm trying to setup deis on a bare-meta cluster. `deisctl start platform` goes fine except `deis-builder` step. When I check logs, I see following:
```
Feb 16 13:35:10 staging-188_40_90_209 sh[27247]: Invalid registry endpoint https://188.40.90.200:5000/v1/: Get https://188.40.90.200:5000/v1/_ping: EOF. If this private registry supports only HTTP or HTTPS with an unknown CA certificate, please add `--insecure-registry 188.40.90.200:5000` to the daemon's arguments. In the case of HTTPS, if you have access to the registry's CA certificate, no need for the flag; simply place the CA certificate at /etc/docker/certs.d/188.40.90.200:5000/ca.crt
```

Why is it trying to connect to https? `registry` runs http:
```
core@staging-188_40_90_200 ~ $ etcdctl get /deis/registry/protocol
http
```
So connect to https obviously fails.
I'm using Deis version 1.3.1 on CoreOS beta.

issue_title: Trouble determining controller URL in Azure
issue_body:
New to Deis, running through the docs to set up an initial base cluster in Azure. I've gotten to the point where Deis seems to be installed and running appropriately; deisctl list gives me a nice long list of loaded, active and running containers across my 3 CoreOS hosts (http://pastebin.com/4kPmVfw4). The docs state that my next step is to register the first user, the superuser, using the deis CLI, using the controller URL provided by "my Deis administrator". I believe my issue is related to the domain that I set in a prior step with deisctl and an inability to reach "http://deis.mydeisdomain.com". In Azure, per the platform instructions in the Deis docs, I created a cloud service container to which my vm hosts are attached. While each host has a unique <hostname>.cloudapp.net, as far as I can tell the only externally resolvable name right now is [containerID].cloudapp.net; going to that URL gets me the Nginx default page of what I assume is the router. And I assume that since it's used likely for every user in Azure at some point that "cloudapp.net" isn't going to cut it here.

I would prefer to do this without purchasing a "real" domain; is that possible in this context? What I've not yet tried is to set up a DNS server locally in my office. Is the only way to do this in Azure to purchase a domain name for the purpose? What's the best/easiest way to get past this and start playing with deploying apps on my test cluster in Azure?

issue_title: AI: Server telemetry channel was not initialized. So persistent storage is turned off. You need to call ServerTelemetryChannel.Initialize(). Currently monitoring will continue but if telemetry cannot be sent it will be dropped.
issue_body:
I'm using the vanilla defaults with Django, installed as shown in the docs.  And every half hour, I get this trace in App Insight:
`
AI: Server telemetry channel was not initialized. So persistent storage is turned off. You need to call ServerTelemetryChannel.Initialize(). Currently monitoring will continue but if telemetry cannot be sent it will be dropped.

As well, this trace also shows up occasionally:

AI: Performance counter is not available in the web app supported list. Counter is \Memory\Available Bytes
`

I've poked around the portal but haven't seen anything that would address this.  Getting these messages makes it seem like something is improperly configured, but like I said, I'm just using the vanilla Django defaults installed per instruction.  Please advise.  Thanks.

Currently our internal format is 

``` Python
{kind: pandas DataFrame with exactly two columns (the value and the id)}

So one example is

{'a':      id  val

The problem is the id column, this information is duplicated. Maybe we can have more nested dictionaries? Like

```
{kind: { id: values} }
```

Here, we could have the values as a plain numpy array

issue_title: Make sure we use a ProcessPoolExecutor for cpu-intensive tasks
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

@carver [run into some issues when using a ProcessPoolExecutor](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/pull/754) as the default executor, and apparently [GvR thinks that's not a good idea anyway](https://groups.google.com/d/msg/python-tulip/91NCCqV4SFs/9McZnfea_VcJ) so we're back to using a ThreadPoolExecutor as the default

### How can it be fixed

Places where we use loop.run_in_executor to offload cpu-intensive tasks must explicitly use a ProcessPoolExecutor

This is because of a malformed call to `re.sub()`:

```python
    return re.sub(r'(\\s|\\n|\\\\)', _unescape, string, re.UNICODE)
```

The 4th argument of `re.sub()` is not the flags, but the count, and `re.UNICODE` has a value of 32. This can be fixed by using a keyword argument:

```python
    return re.sub(r'(\\s|\\n|\\\\)', _unescape, string, flags=re.UNICODE)
```

issue_title: Lower version win64 pkgs preferred to newer noarch. 4.3.17 regression on Windows
issue_body:
With `conda 4.3.17` the solver prefers Windows platform-specific packages over `noarch` even if the `noarch` version has a higher version number. This is not observable on a Linux and also works fine on Windows for `conda 4.3.16`.
See the following:
```batch

(C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64) C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64\maba>conda create --dry-run -n tmp -c genomeinformatics amplikyzer2
Fetching package metadata ...............
Solving package specifications: .

Package plan for installation in environment C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64\envs\tmp:

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    amplikyzer2:     1.2.0-0            genomeinformatics
    cycler:          0.10.0-py36_0
    icu:             57.1-vc14_0                          [vc14]
    jpeg:            9b-vc14_0                            [vc14]
    libpng:          1.6.27-vc14_0                        [vc14]
    llvmlite:        0.17.0-py36_0
    matplotlib:      2.0.0-np112py36_0
    mkl:             2017.0.1-0
    numba:           0.32.0-np112py36_0
    numpy:           1.12.1-py36_0
    openssl:         1.0.2k-vc14_0                        [vc14]
    pip:             9.0.1-py36_1
    pyparsing:       2.1.4-py36_0
    pyqt:            5.6.0-py36_2
    python:          3.6.1-0
    python-dateutil: 2.6.0-py36_0
    pytz:            2017.2-py36_0
    qt:              5.6.2-vc14_3                         [vc14]
    setuptools:      27.2.0-py36_1
    sip:             4.18-py36_0
    six:             1.10.0-py36_0
    tk:              8.5.18-vc14_0                        [vc14]
    vs2015_runtime:  14.0.25123-0
    wheel:           0.29.0-py36_0
    zlib:            1.2.8-vc14_3                         [vc14]


(C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64) C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64\maba>conda --version
conda 4.3.16
```
`amplikyzer2 1.2.0` is the most recent version and uses `noarch: python`.
After updating to `conda 4.3.17`, an outdated version is selected:
```batch
(C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64) C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64\maba>conda update conda
Fetching package metadata ...............
Solving package specifications: .

Package plan for installation in environment C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64:

Proceed ([y]/n)?

conda-4.3.17-p 100% |###############################| Time: 0:00:00   2.42 MB/s

(C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64) C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64\maba>conda create --dry-run -n tmp -c genomeinformatics amplikyzer2
Fetching package metadata ...............
Solving package specifications: .

Package plan for installation in environment C:\home\maba\conda\miniconda-win64\envs\tmp:

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    amplikyzer2:     1.1.2-0            genomeinformatics
    cycler:          0.10.0-py36_0
    icu:             57.1-vc14_0                          [vc14]
    jpeg:            9b-vc14_0                            [vc14]
    libpng:          1.6.27-vc14_0                        [vc14]
    llvmlite:        0.17.0-py36_0
    matplotlib:      2.0.0-np112py36_0
    mkl:             2017.0.1-0
    numba:           0.32.0-np112py36_0
    numpy:           1.12.1-py36_0
    openssl:         1.0.2k-vc14_0                        [vc14]
    pip:             9.0.1-py36_1
    pyparsing:       2.1.4-py36_0
    pyqt:            5.6.0-py36_2
    python:          3.6.1-0
    python-dateutil: 2.6.0-py36_0
    pytz:            2017.2-py36_0
    qt:              5.6.2-vc14_3                         [vc14]
    setuptools:      27.2.0-py36_1
    sip:             4.18-py36_0
    six:             1.10.0-py36_0
    tk:              8.5.18-vc14_0                        [vc14]
    vs2015_runtime:  14.0.25123-0
    wheel:           0.29.0-py36_0
    zlib:            1.2.8-vc14_3                         [vc14]

```
`amplikyzer2 1.1.2-0` is the last platform-specific Windows build, but of course is superseded by `1.2.0`.

The same also happens for `conda install amplikyzer2` and `conda update amplikyzer2`. If the version is explicitly given, e.g., `conda install amplikyzer2=1.2.0`, the newest version is correctly installed. Running `conda update amplikyzer2` afterwards will try to downgrade to `1.1.2-0` again.

issue_title: Can't create Python 2.7.x env inside a VirtualEnv
issue_body:
I have the use case that I need `conda` installed inside a `VirtualEnv`. This schema was working until yesterday that I tried to rebuild the environment.

issue_title: Using system install of conda
issue_body:
I'm experimenting with using a system install of conda/conda-env in /usr/bin.  The problem I'm running into is:
~~~~~
$ source /opt/anaconda/envs/scipy/bin/activate /opt/anaconda/envs/scipy
discarding /usr/bin from PATH
prepending /opt/anaconda/envs/scipy/bin to PATH
~~~~~
In this situation we don't really have a "root" environment, or at least not one managed by conda.  I know this a strange use case, but I think a potentially useful one.

issue_title: Permission Errors in create_link
issue_body:
This might be a bug in conda, but definitely a bug in packaging.  But it complicates the multi-user feature of conda-4.3.  The latest anaconda 4.2.0 release is not compatible with the multi-user cache features.

The packaging bug is summarized here.
https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues/issues/1301

On the conda side: create_link doesn't seem to account for permissions errors (or, maybe that handling happens at a different level).  Should there be some error handling here to account for the situation described in https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues/issues/1301? Conda could even check during the extraction phase that all files are world readable, and if not inform the user and change the permissions to world-readable.

Or, as the poster in 1301 suggests, maybe such info should be given to the packager at the conda-build level, ensuring that all files in a package are world-readable and warning the packager that the resulting package may not be usable in multi-user environments.

issue_title: pip writes into a cache that is not environment specific leading to problems when attempting to rebuild.
issue_body:
to reproduce have an environment that uses a pip package that depends on a shared library.
```
conda env create -f environment.yml
conda env remove -n {env}
mv lib{foo.so.5.0.0,foo.so.5.0.1}
ln -s libfoo.so libfoo.so.5.0.1
conda env create -f environment.yml 
```

this will fail due to the pip cache in the user's home directory referencing the old shared library.
conda clean --all doesn't fix this because it doesn't touch the pip cache.
conda needs to move the pip cache into the environment directory when installing pip packages so that a clean, or remove will properly remove said references.

issue_title: Make pre-/post-link behavior more atomic
issue_body:
I understand that conda can't guarantee a pre/post-link script doesn't break anything before failing. Conda can at least ensure that any changes it made to the environment prior to the failure are undone (particularly the unlinking of other packages). It would mean that **if a pre/post link script needs to cancel the installation cleanly, it can do so by cleaning up any mess it made and trusting that conda cleans any changes it made**.

Really, conda only needs to keep track of what it has unlinked and should re-link it after a pre/post script exit.

issue_title: envs_dirs not being respected by conda create
issue_body:
I've seen some other issues with envs_dirs mentioned but they seem to address something slightly different. 

My setup: running anaconda3/4.3.1 with conda/4.3.1. This is installed to a central readable but not writable directory. I want to enable a shared environment folder.

I created a .condarc in that directory and included 

```
envs_dirs:
 - /data/conda

```
When I run conda info, it confirms that both that the .condarc is being used and /data/conda is the first "envs directories" entry.  So far, so good.

However, when I do a conda create (e.g. conda create -n test python=3.5) it tries to install it in ~/.conda/envs/test. 

I've confirmed the /data/conda is writeable and I can create an environment there using "conda create -p /data/conda/test python=3.5". However, I don't see why it would not try to create the environment there by default. When I use the -vvv option, it doesn't ever seem to be checking for /data/conda.

issue_title: explicit() doesn't remove existing package first => try_hard_link() fails => symlinks are used => relative RPATHs in executables do not work.
issue_body:
I was looking into why symlinks were being used everywhere as it was preventing me from testing packages installed with a full path.

It seems that `explicit()` (or something in that callstack) doesn't remove the existing package first.

This means that `try_hard_link()` will fail because `dst` exists and `assert not isfile(dst), dst` will fire. This gets caught in `explicit()` which (I guess) makes the assumption that hardlinks are not possible.

Conda then goes off and symlinks everything which causes `RPATH` to fail in executables as symlinks are followed before the RPATH is canonicalized/normalized.

I'm not sure if there's a reason not to unlink first? But this bug also gives us the opportunity to mark executables as such and *never* symlink them.

issue_title: Serializer don't support time.struct_time type
Confusion at tracelog
issue_body:
Hi all;

During a debug trace, a tracer.info message fooled me for a while and caused me to loose some time before figuring out what the problem actually was. It made me think that I was making the requests to localhost instead of the remote host;

In connection/base.py:73

`tracer.info("curl -X%s 'http://localhost:9200%s' -d '%s'", method, path, _pretty_json(body) if body else '')`

"localhost:9200" needs to be replaced with %s -> self.host I believe,

Shall I create a PR for this?

https://github.com/sinanislekdemir/elasticsearch-py/commit/6624fc42bc542a85c3faeca73984180bba33c019

Python (Linux) 2.7.13 (default, Jan 13 2017, 10:15:16) 
[GCC 6.3.1 20161221 (Red Hat 6.3.1-1)]
```

**OS Version:** Linux Fedora 24 (4.9.13-101.fc24.x86_64)

**Shell Type:** bash

### Description

When running a zone import e.g.:

```
az network dns zone import -g my-rg -n mydomain.com -f mydomain-zonefile.txt
```

where `mydomain-zonefile.txt` has the following content:

```
@     600     IN      SOA     ignored.com. hostmaster.mydomain.com. (
                                2017040400
                                28800
                                7200
                                604800
                                600
                                )

test    3600    IN      A       2.2.2.2
```

The relative domain record "test" is imported into Azure DNS as absolute value `test.mydomain.com` instead of relative value `test`, meaning that lookup `test.mydomain.com.mydomain.com` works and `test.mydomain.com` fails.

issue_title: Suggestion: Prompt for confirmation on disk delete
issue_body:
When deleting a disk with `az disk delete`, there is no prompt that you wish to continue. When deleting a resource group with `az group delete`, you are prompted or can pass `--yes` to continue without a prompt.

Deleting an NSG rule with `az nsg rule delete` also doesn't prompt for confirmation, though deleting a disk is potentially a more destructive process. There are likely other similar examples.

I'd recommend that at the least, a confirmation is added to `az disk delete`, and ideally some amount of consistency across potentially destructive commands that delete resources.

issue_title: webapp: update the create command logics to avoid scm shutdown
issue_body:
There is a known racing issue between appsettings update and sourcecontrol update. In a nutshell, updating appsettings returns immediately,. however it may take 5-10s to take affect and could shutdown the SCM site. So if CLI submits source control change too soon, it will fail for internal server error.
The mitigating option at client side is to move all appsetting changes into webapp create, which is a right thing to do regardless

issue_title: Regression: Multi chunk transfer hangs as merging chunks fails
issue_body:
### Description

This is caused by https://github.com/Azure/azure-data-lake-store-python/commit/4b3171d87fe0efeadcd688a561b67144d229c288 which removed `_submit` to achieve better control of done callback behavior and avoid a race condition. 

However `_submit` was called when merging chunks and now fails.

The behavior is that the transfer will hang after the error. I'm not clear why it hangs instead of actually bubbling the error up (that may be due to a separate issue).

I'll submit a patch once I finish testing. Note that this affects latest release of the CLI.
```
azure.datalake.store.transfer : Chunks transferred
azure.datalake.store.transfer : Merging file: transferring
concurrent.futures : exception calling callback for <Future at 0x10faa1610 state=finished returned tuple>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/clehene/work/azure-cli/env/lib/python2.7/site-packages/concurrent/futures/_base.py", line 301, in _invoke_callbacks
    callback(self)
  File "/Users/clehene/work/azure-cli/env/lib/python2.7/site-packages/azure/datalake/store/transfer.py", line 434, in _update
    merge_future = self._submit(
AttributeError: 'ADLTransferClient' object has no attribute '_submit'
```

Interrupting the process after the exception will "wake it":

```
^Cazure.datalake.store.transfer : <azure.datalake.store.transfer.ADLTransferClient object at 0x104fba290> suspended and persisted
azure.datalake.store.transfer : Shutting down worker threads
azure.datalake.store.transfer : Shutdown complete
Application event 'Application.TransformResults' with event data {'event_data': {'result': None}}
Application event 'Application.FilterResults' with event data {'event_data': {'result': None}}
```

---

### Reproduction Steps
* Upload a file and make sure the chunk size is smaller than the file size 

### Environment summary

**SDK Version:** What version of the SDK  are you using? (pip show azure-datalake-store)
```
Name: azure-datalake-store
Version: 0.0.15
```

issue_title: Private key recognition hook is not accurate
issue_body:
Hi Team! 

I just discovered your work, and it is fantastic. Many thanks for putting this together, it is very useful.

I have a use case where code is extracting private keys, and contains lines such as

```
String keyContents = base64Content.VersionData.tostring();
keyContents = keyContents.replace('-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----', '');
keyContents = keyContents.replace('-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----', '');
keyContents = keyContents.replace('\n', '');
```
This is caught by the private key discoverer and it makes the tests fail. 

Technically, private keys or certs would have a pattern that is 
(-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----)( base64 stuff)(-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----)

The current code seems to just check if the words "BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY" appears in the code, which is seems slightly weak. 

As a result, I am using a combination of pre-commit and [Talisman](https://github.com/thoughtworks/talisman) but I would love to have a single code base for everything. 

Best,
Sam

issue_title: 2.83 tag changed
issue_body:
The 2.83 tag was created on Sunday night, but then it was modified on Monday. It's my understanding that tags are supposed to be immutable references to specific points in the code and should not be changed. It wreaks havoc on downstream users who package the code. I would recommend using patch release numbering (2.83.1) rather than modify the original tag.

I think @sherm1 did the same thing recently with simbody, so you're not alone. But I think I complained to him too.

It's done now, probably no need to revert it, but I would recommend patch release numbering for the future.

issue_title: Transition matrix stabilization results in un-normalized transition matrices
issue_body:
In base.py, the transition matrix is calculated from the sufficient statistics and then normalized. However, in the follow line, the transition matrix update is done element-by-element, ignoring cells which are less than eps. The result is an un-normalized transition matrix if fit() stops after this update, which breaks, e.g., score().

I just eliminated the eps - check, which means that some cells of the transition matrix will effectively go to zero. I'm not sure what the "right" thing to do is.

````
        if 't' in params:
            transmat_ = self.transmat_prior - 1.0 + stats['trans']
            normalize(transmat_, axis=1)
            self.transmat_ = np.where(self.transmat_ <= np.finfo(float).eps,
                                      self.transmat_, transmat_)
````

X = np.concatenate([X1, X2])
lengths = [len(X1), len(X2)]
model = hmm.GaussianHMM(n_components=3).fit(X, lengths)  

My question is how to interpret the sampling from this fit, which is just one observation sequence? For example model.sample(100)?

issue_title: The documentation on "Background tasks" is wrong
issue_body:
## Long story short

In the example provided here: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/docs/web.rst#background-tasks we add a new key to the application which is a background task.

If you activate the warnings using `-Wdefault` and execute the script, you will get:

```
/tmp/b.py:15: DeprecationWarning: Changing state of started or joined application is deprecated
```

## Expected behaviour

Not sure. Not having the warning? The example doesn't look bad, only `make_handler` is called before the `on_startup` signal which freezes the app before the signal is sent.

## Actual behaviour

When the example in the doc is executed, you get the warning.

## Steps to reproduce

I made this variant to test it out:

```
import asyncio
from aiohttp import web


async def listen_to_redis(app):
    try:
        await asyncio.sleep(200)
    except asyncio.CancelledError:
        pass
    finally:
        print("cleanup")


async def start_background_tasks(app):
    app['redis_listener'] = app.loop.create_task(listen_to_redis(app))


async def cleanup_background_tasks(app):
    app['redis_listener'].cancel()
    await app['redis_listener']


app = web.Application()
app.on_startup.append(start_background_tasks)
app.on_cleanup.append(cleanup_background_tasks)
web.run_app(app)
```

And run: `python -Wdefault <script>.py`

## Your environment

Python 3.6, Arch Linux

issue_title: 5.0 Release Schedule
issue_body:
I wanted to open an issue to track and discuss the planned release schedule for 5.0. Here is what I would propose:

* Cut an alpha release once all PRs associated with open 5.0 milestone issues are merged. There is talk about that being roughly a week from now and I think that sounds about right.
* Finish any UI/UX design review (see #2142) during the alpha cycle. The only caveat is that experience shows that these UI/UX design reviews take more time that initially expected. Because of that, I think we need to plan to have a longer alpha cycle that may involve non-trivial fixes to the new UI/UX. I don't *know* this is needed, but in the past it always has been needed for new UI/UX.
* Cut an additional alpha release once the UI/UX design stuff is done.
* Wait at least a few days.
* Final release.

issue_title: get_value_in: support of float values
issue_body:
`get_range_endpoints()` uses `random.randint()` to produce random numbers while creating mock objects.

The issue is that `FloatType` fails to create mock objects, if it has float values for `min_value` and `max_value` properties.

E.g., having field

``` python

delay = FloatType(
    min_value=1.0,
    max_value=30.0,
    default=10.0,
    required=True,
)
```

will raise an error

```ServerConfig.get_mock_object().to_primitive()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<input>", line 1, in <module>
    ServerConfig.get_mock_object().to_primitive()
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/models.py", line 378, in get_mock_object
    values[name] = field.mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 323, in mock
    return self._mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/compound.py", line 115, in _mock
    return self.model_class.get_mock_object(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/models.py", line 378, in get_mock_object
    values[name] = field.mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 323, in mock
    return self._mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/compound.py", line 115, in _mock
    return self.model_class.get_mock_object(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/models.py", line 378, in get_mock_object
    values[name] = field.mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 323, in mock
    return self._mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/compound.py", line 115, in _mock
    return self.model_class.get_mock_object(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/models.py", line 378, in get_mock_object
    values[name] = field.mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 323, in mock
    return self._mock(context)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 450, in _mock
    return get_value_in(self.min_value, self.max_value)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/site-packages/schematics/types/base.py", line 71, in get_value_in
    *get_range_endpoints(min_length, max_length, padding, required_length))
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/random.py", line 218, in randint
    return self.randrange(a, b+1)
  File "/Users/alex/.virtualenvs/sandbox/lib/python3.4/random.py", line 182, in randrange
    raise ValueError("non-integer arg 1 for randrange()")
ValueError: non-integer arg 1 for randrange()
```

Obviously, `random.randint` cannot be used for creating random floats. `random.uniform` must do this job.

`get_value_in` is also used by `StringType` and so on. The most simple approach is to select random generator basing on type of `min_length` and `max_length` parameters, e.g.:

``` python

def get_value_in(min_length, max_length, padding=0, required_length=0):
    generator = (
        random.uniform
        if isinstance(min_length, float) or isinstance(max_length, float) else
        random.randint
    )
    return generator(
        *get_range_endpoints(min_length, max_length, padding, required_length))
```

But, IMHO, this is a bit dirty way. It's better to specify proper generator in type/field definition. This will also allow user to set own generator, e.g. from `faker` library.

Thanks

issue_title: planemo lint XSD check unaware of legacy parameter attribute default_value
issue_body:
While ``<param value="..." ...>`` is prefered, Galaxy still supports ``<param default_value="..." ...>`` for a ``ColumnListParameter``, as can be seen here:

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/tools/parameters/basic.py#L1005

``` python
        # Legacy style default value specification...
        self.default_value = input_source.get( "default_value", None )
        if self.default_value is None:
            # Newer style... more in line with other parameters.
            self.default_value = input_source.get( "value", None )
        if self.default_value is not None:
            self.default_value = ColumnListParameter._strip_c( self.default_value )
```

The XSD should be aware of this legacy attribute, which currently causes lint failures, e.g. https://travis-ci.org/peterjc/galaxy_blast/jobs/167595623

```
+Linting tool /home/travis/build/peterjc/galaxy_blast/tools/blastxml_to_top_descr/blastxml_to_top_descr.xml
Applying linter tests... CHECK
.. CHECK: 2 test(s) found.
Applying linter output... CHECK
.. INFO: 1 outputs found.
Applying linter inputs... CHECK
.. INFO: Found 7 input parameters.
Applying linter help... CHECK
.. CHECK: Tool contains help section.
.. CHECK: Help contains valid reStructuredText.
Applying linter general... CHECK
.. CHECK: Tool defines a version [0.1.2].
.. CHECK: Tool defines a name [BLAST top hit descriptions].
.. CHECK: Tool defines an id [blastxml_to_top_descr].
Applying linter command... CHECK
.. INFO: Command uses deprecated 'interpreter' attribute.
.. INFO: Tool contains a command with interpreter of type [python].
Applying linter citations... CHECK
.. CHECK: Found 1 likely valid citations.
Applying linter tool_xsd... FAIL
.. ERROR: Invalid tmp6TctTh found. Errors [/tmp/tmp6TctTh:24:0:ERROR:SCHEMASV:SCHEMAV_CVC_COMPLEX_TYPE_3_2_1: Element 'option', attribute 'select': The attribute 'select' is not allowed.
/tmp/tmp6TctTh:32:0:ERROR:SCHEMASV:SCHEMAV_CVC_COMPLEX_TYPE_3_2_1: Element 'param', attribute 'default_value': The attribute 'default_value' is not allowed.
/tmp/tmp6TctTh:33:0:ERROR:SCHEMASV:SCHEMAV_CVC_COMPLEX_TYPE_3_2_1: Element 'param', attribute 'default_value': The attribute 'default_value' is not allowed.
/tmp/tmp6TctTh:34:0:ERROR:SCHEMASV:SCHEMAV_CVC_COMPLEX_TYPE_3_2_1: Element 'param', attribute 'default_value': The attribute 'default_value' is not allowed.]
Applying linter shed_metadata... CHECK
.. INFO: Found all shed metadata fields required for automated repository creation and/or updates.
Failed linting
```

See https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/commit/9128311a607ef0c04d6db17060c7762accbe345c (originally only ``default_value`` was supported) and the option to use the more common ``value`` added in https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/commit/40f19d2a0fd4ccfe52b8b13f1a5de75d623951ac

issue_title: Aliases not coming through in giraffez.Cmd.execute
issue_body:
When running the following in 2.0.7:

```python
sql = """
SELECT
    field_1 AS alias_1,
    field_2 AS alias_2
FROM
    db.table
"""

with giraffez.Cmd() as cmd:
    res = cmd.execute(sql)
    all_recs = list(res.to_dict())
```

`all_recs` looks like `[{'FIELD_1': 123, 'FIELD_2': None}, {'FIELD_1': 456, 'FIELD_2': 123}, ...]`.  Contrast with behaviour in version 1.1.7 on the same SQL:

```python
with giraffez.Cmd() as cmd:
    res = cmd.execute_one(sql)
    all_recs = list(res.to_json())
```

and `all_recs` had the "safe" column names, and lower-cased to boot, like `[{'alias_1': 123, 'alias_2': None}, {'alias_1': 456, 'alias_2': 123}, ...]`

issue_title: Export right-pads CHAR types with extra spaces
issue_body:
In using the `giraffez.Export` module, I've found that CHAR(x) types get padded on the right with a number of spaces equal to the the length of the field (in addition to any blank characters that exist at the end of the value that don't fill the full _x_ characters). I believe expected behavior here would be to trim the value as happens with VARCHAR types, and the additional spaces equal to the length of the field are clearly unwelcome.

Here's an example that I think should be reproducible:

```python
>>> with giraffez.Load(host=host, username=username, password=password) as load:
...     load.execute("""create multiset table giraffez_issue (
...         id_var int,
...         variable_field varchar(10),
...         fixed_field char(4),
...         floaty decimal(6, 2)
...     ) primary index (id_var)""")
...     
...     load.insert('giraffez_issue', [
...         (1, 'alpha', 'A1', 1.1),
...         (2, 'beta', 'B', 2.2),
...         (3, 'gamma', 'C3PO', 3.0)
...     ])
... 

defaultdict(<class 'int'>, {'count': 3})
>>> with giraffez.Export('giraffez_issue', host=host, username=username,
...                      password=password, delimiter='|', null='') as export:
...     for row in export.results():
...         print('<<{row}>>'.format(row=row))
... 
<<1|alpha|A1      |1.10>>
<<3|gamma|C3PO    |3.00>>
<<2|beta|B       |2.20>>
```

It seems something has gone wrong with the last update, our charms crash when we remove a relation.

```
root@juju-4209d8-68:/var/lib/juju/agents/unit-gateway-test-0/charm# hooks/upstream-relation-departed 
Traceback (most recent call last):

issue_title: Missing urlencode of fact values
issue_body:
When looking up nodes by fact values using `db.facts(name, value)`, pypuppetdb doesn't encode the value in the request URL, which results in PuppetDB returning a "404 Not Found" error.

For example:
```
for r in db.facts('macaddress', '02:42:ec:94:80:f0'):
    print(r)
```
triggers the following exception:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test_mac_lookup.py", line 10, in <module>
    for r in db.facts('macaddress', '02:42:ec:94:80:f0'):
  File "/home/olavmo/test_mac_lookup/.ve/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pypuppetdb/api/__init__.py", line 531, in facts
    facts = self._query('facts', path=path, **kwargs)
  File "/home/olavmo/test_mac_lookup/.ve/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pypuppetdb/api/__init__.py", line 332, in _query
    r.raise_for_status()
  File "/home/olavmo/test_mac_lookup/.ve/lib/python3.4/site-packages/requests/models.py", line 840, in raise_for_status
    raise HTTPError(http_error_msg, response=self)
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 404 Client Error: Not Found for url: https://puppetdb.uninett.no:8081/pdb/query/v4/facts/macaddress/02:42:ec:94:80:f0
```

If we manually escape the value, it works:
```
for r in db.facts('macaddress', '02%3a42%3aec%3a94%3a80%3af0'):
    print(r)
```
prints:
```
macaddress/dlolamor14.ikt.uninett.no
```

issue_title: Automatically optimize imports

PyCharm has such a feature, but it doesn't do it as precisely as flake8-import-order can.

I suggest either adding an "Auto-optimize" feature that modifies the order the imports in the top-level import block (via --in-place flag), or an "optimized suggestion" that is outputted.

issue_title: apk.show() isn't returning intent filters
issue_body:
### Describe what you wanted to do

By calling androguard/androguard/core/bytecodes/apk.APK('file.apk').show(), no intent-filters are returned in the printout.

- is this error specific to a single file?
No
- if possible give a minimal working example where the error happens
For use in androlyze.py -s
`from androguard.core.bytecodes import apk
a = apk.APK('example.apk')
a.show()`

### Describe what you expected
Intent filters to be printed after the name of activities, etc

### Describe what actually happened
They're not printed.

### System Information

- Androguard Version: master
- Python Version: 3.5.2
- Operating System Ubuntu 16.04.3

### Resolution
The following line submits bytes to get_intent_filters. These bytes need to be decoded with .decode
https://github.com/androguard/androguard/blob/9f88ec774ec9b089f060e075785fe091465d8e22/androguard/core/bytecodes/apk.py#L986

I'll submit PR and fix for this and some other issues this or next week.

issue_title: add IP Intelligence endpoint to ASM policy
Add support for saving config
issue_body:
Need to save BIG-IP configuration. Using the BIG-IP REST API, POST JSON {'command': 'save'} to /sys/config. Here's the complete implementation from LBaaS v1:

    def save_config(self):
        """ Save the bigip configuration """
        request_url = self.bigip.icr_url + '/sys/config'
        payload = dict()
        payload['command'] = 'save'
        response = self.bigip.icr_session.post(
            request_url, data=json.dumps(payload),
            timeout=const.CONNECTION_TIMEOUT)
        if response.status_code < 400:
            return True
        else:
            Log.error('config', response.text)
            raise exceptions.BigIPClusterConfigSaveFailure(response.text)
        return False

issue_title: condition alleviation bug
issue_body: Shows up in particular when the condition is high and hence the smallest eigenvalue is increasing by orders of magnitude _and_ eigenvectors are not aligned in the coordinate system. The bug shows up for both, alleviation in coordinates and resetting C=I. ``self.alleviate_conditioning_in_coordinates(1e2)`` was used in this example to see both in on run: ```python import functools import cma f = functools.partial(cma.ff.elli, cond=1e28) frot = cma.fitness_transformations.Rotated(f) es = cma.fmin2(frot, [1] + 3 * [0], 0.1, {'tolconditioncov':False, 'seed': 4, })[1] ```

issue_title: Empty translations
issue_body:
Some subtranslations are empty after commit 83dc5e45cea64f6af407532f00a92539e0a3db92

This commit introduced empty subtranslations (e.g. sk_SK for slovak language), which are loaded instead of main translations (e.g. sk).

It looks like the same problem as in django-cms - https://github.com/divio/django-cms/issues/5334

Deleting empty translations solved problem.

issue_title: date_from and date_to should be required=False
issue_body:
These fields:
https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/1.0.1/oscar/apps/dashboard/reports/forms.py#L22

should be set to `required=False`

Because the base `ReportGenerator` class is capable of handling form submissions without a date range by checking if `self.date_range_field_name` is set to `None`. 
https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/1.0.1/oscar/apps/dashboard/reports/reports.py#L56

issue_title: ExpiryMonthField raises AttributeError inappropriately
issue_body:
Similar to https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/issues/2006

I'll submit a PR briefly.

issue_title: Enable apache-conf-test in travis
Manual WebRoot Authentication
issue_body:
Hello,

I've been looking at enrolling some of our company websites for LetEncrypt certificates as part of the BETA and we had a slight special use case.

We have compliance requirements which mean we:
* Can't allow the LetsEncrypt software to run on the reverse proxy nodes.
* Can't run anything on port 80/443 other than nginx serving our sites, availability is quite important.
* Need replication to a group of servers before validation, it could pick any one of them.

My solution was to combine the manual and webroot plugins, gist of my change to webroot.py here: https://gist.github.com/iamacarpet/fb4a8f424390c0352e93

To use, you simply:
* Run letsencrypt with the webroot plugin.
* It'll tell you when it's written the files and ask you to install them.
* Copy from the local webroot folder to your remote server(s).
* Make sure web server is configured - will include nginx config below.
* Hit enter back at the letencrypt script & it will validate.
* Install certs to remote server(s) as required.

nginx config:
`location /.well-known/acme-challenge {
    alias /var/data/letsencrypt/.well-known/acme-challenge;
    location ~ /.well-known/acme-challenge/(.*) {
        add_header Content-Type application/jose+json;
    }
}`
Borrowed from here: https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/using-the-webroot-domain-verification-method/1445/6

I feel it would be good as it's own plugin as editing the webroot plugin isn't great, but I'm afraid we don't have the time to complete at the moment, so thought I'd share in it's current state :).

Regards,
IAmACarpet

issue_title: Batch consumer ?
issue_body:
Is there a way where I can get batch of messages instead of a single message. 

What I'm trying to achieve is to get batch of messages and convert them to Python dataframe using from_json, and then do some operations in the dataframe and store the parquet files to s3. 

The problem here is I can't just wait make the dataframe from only few messages. It has to be atleast 2L messages. 

For this I can't iterate over 2L times, and poll the consume to get one by one message and store it and then process further. 

Instead if there is a way where we can tell the consumer poll to return next 100 messages, it will be good. So it should return 100 messages if 100 messages are available or the n number of messages that are available.  

I think this is general use case where most of the time we don't process message by message but a batch of messages.

issue_title: It's impossible to parse ISO-style and European-style dates at the same time
issue_body:
I've got the following two representation for the 1st December: "1.12.2017" and "2017-12-01". The first is European-style (that means day first) and the second is in ISO format. 

I failed at finding an invocation of dateutil.parser.parse() that works on both inputs. The crucial parameter seems to be dayfirst. If set to True the European-style input is parsed correctly and if set to False the ISO-style date is parsed correctly.

After thoroughly studying the documentation I've come to the conclusion that this is currently impossible to do and constitutes a gap in parser functionality.

issue_title: __all__ not defined on root package
issue_body:
Hi, I don't know if this is a problem or intentionally planed, but because of namespace conflicts, we typically import the base package and not deep into it. For example:
```
import dateutil

dateutil.parser.parse('Fri Jun 30 12:00:56 EDT 2017')
```

However this appears to fail with the error:
> AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'parser'

The solution appears to be: `from dateutil import parser` and then call parser, I'm guessing this is because the `__all__` in the root `__init__.py` doesn't exist thus not listing any packages. Is this intended behavior? Should I just use my above workaround?
Thanks!

issue_title: Rename package name on PyPI
issue_body:
It's strange that the package name on PyPI is `python-dateutil`. It's implied that PyPI hosts only python packages, why prefixing the name with `python-`?

`dateutil` is available: https://pypi.python.org/pypi?%3Aaction=search&term=dateutil&submit=search

You could upload the newest version with the new name, so that when people will upgrade it, they will change package name. Or support two identical packages for several years -- one with the current `python-dateutil` name and a new one `dateutil`.

I think the current package name is causing this in PyCharm:

issue_title: Incorrect mapping of SNV to insertion on GRCh37
issue_body:
Variant: [NM_001145026.1:c.715A>G](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/156332)
hgvs version: 1.2.1
data_version: uta_20170629

In hgvs-shell, when mapped to genomic HGVS, the variant becomes an insertion:

```python
hgvs = 'NM_001145026.1:c.715A>G'
vc = hp.parse_hgvs_variant(hgvs)
vg = am37.c_to_g(vc)
print(vg)
# NC_000012.11:g.80860615_80860616insGAGAATAGTGAATCTTTTTTATGGAGTACAGCCAGCCCTTCTCCAACCCTTGGTGGAGTTACACCTCCATCGCGTACCACACATTCATCAAGCACGTTGACACAGAATGAGATCAGCTCTGTGTGGAAAGAGCCTATCAGTTTTGTAGTGACACACTTGAG

I suspect this is due to mismatch of the transcript NM_001145026.1 on GRCh37, but mapping a SNV to insertion does not sound right anyway.

issue_title: Marshmallow ext: Name should be set to dump_to even if load_from is not specified
issue_body:
At the moment, name is changed if both load_from and dump_to is set and are equal, as per this code:
```
def _observed_name(field, name):
    """Adjust field name to reflect `dump_to` and `load_from` attributes.
    :param Field field: A marshmallow field.
    :param str name: Field name
    :rtype: str
    """
    # use getattr in case we're running against older versions of marshmallow.
    dump_to = getattr(field, 'dump_to', None)
    load_from = getattr(field, 'load_from', None)
    if load_from != dump_to:
        return name
    return dump_to or name
```
This behaviour causes schema to look bad and does not make sense.
Name should be set to dump_to even if load_from is not specified.

issue_title: [marshmallow plugin] Inspect validators

field = fields.Int(validate=validate.Range(min=3, max=42)

prop = field2property(field)
assert prop['minimum'] == 3
assert prop['maximum'] == 42
```

issue_title: skip_on_fields_errors doesn't work with class Meta include
Error on serialization of custom class - if attr is callable it is assumed its constructor takes no arguments
issue_body:
The issue got introduced in 2.0.0b5 by the following diff and is still present in 2.7.1:

```
--- marshmallow-2.0.0b4/utils.py        2016-04-12 18:04:58.209260000 -0600
+++ marshmallow-2.0.0b5/utils.py        2016-04-12 18:06:52.287353000 -0600
@@ -316,7 +316,8 @@
         return obj[key]
     except (KeyError, AttributeError, IndexError, TypeError):
         try:
-            return getattr(obj, key)
+            attr = getattr(obj, key)
+            return attr() if callable(attr) else attr
         except AttributeError:
             return default
     return default
```

While trying to traverse and hence serialize certain objects, it might encounter an object which can't be indexed, calls the exception handler which tests it being callable. If so, it does.

In my scenario the object is a class which constructor requires an argument, which the attr() call doesn't provide. In that case it fails with the following backtrace (version 2.0.0b5):

```

    **kwargs

    index=(index if index_errors else None)
  File "/home/mirko/projects/ims.new.new/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/marshalling.py", line 64, in call_and_store
    value = getter_func(data)
  File "/home/mirko/projects/ims.new.new/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/marshalling.py", line 128, in <lambda>
    getter = lambda d: field_obj.serialize(attr_name, d, accessor=accessor)
  File "/home/mirko/projects/ims.new.new/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/fields.py", line 216, in serialize
    value = self.get_value(attr, obj, accessor=accessor)
  File "/home/mirko/projects/ims.new.new/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/fields.py", line 166, in get_value
    return accessor_func(check_key, obj)
  File "/home/mirko/projects/ims.new.new/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/utils.py", line 303, in get_value
    return _get_value_for_keys(key.split('.'), obj, default)
  File "/home/mirko/projects/ims.new.new/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/utils.py", line 308, in _get_value_for_keys
    return _get_value_for_key(keys[0], obj, default)

issue_title: Add request parsing; webargs; integrate flask-smore?

- [ ] Add request parsing by integrating with `webargs`, which now uses marshmallow. This might be as simple as providing the [use_schema](https://github.com/sloria/webargs/blob/dev/examples/schema_example.py) decorator, which allows using a `Schema` for both input and output and a `marshal_with` decorator (just output).
- [ ] Merge [flask-smore](https://github.com/jmcarp/flask-smore)? Would integrate with the above request parsing module and provide automatic swagger spec generation

issue_title: Bug on render() method concerning renderer_context
issue_body:
Hello, we are using this package and ran into an issue with our unit tests where it seems that the rest_framework APIRequestFactory which is behind the test client does not pass in a `renderer_context`:

```
    def render(self, data, accepted_media_type=None, renderer_context=None):

>       view = renderer_context.get("view", None)
E       AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get'

/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/rest_framework_json_api/renderers.py:419: AttributeError
```
I think you could fix it by doing `renderer_context={}`

The code from this library will break at the following lines which are not guarded properly:

https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/blob/develop/rest_framework_json_api/renderers.py#L419

also:
https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/blob/develop/rest_framework_json_api/renderers.py#L401
https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/blob/develop/rest_framework_json_api/renderers.py#L420

Here is the corresponding rest_framework.test line that you can see does not include `renderer_context` as an argument:

https://github.com/tomchristie/django-rest-framework/blob/master/rest_framework/test.py#L183

Let me know if this makes sense or if I'm off course!

Thanks

issue_title: Value keys formatted with `inflection.underscore`
issue_body:
I understand the desire to format all attribute _keys_ using `inflection`, but am curious as to why we need to format attribute _value_ keys to `inflection.underscore`.

e.g. This means that for every JSONField, the all of the keys in the field's object _value_ will be converted from uppercase to lowercase.  This seems like excessive mangling if one needs to validate _value_ keys using case sensitive strings in a custom validator.

Seems the issue is presented [here](https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/blob/develop/rest_framework_json_api/parsers.py#L32) in combination with [here](https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/blob/develop/rest_framework_json_api/utils.py#L126-L128).

issue_title: Allow classmethods as config scopes and captured functions
issue_body:
Here's a small example of what I'd like to be able to do. The real usecase is far more complex:
```python
from sacred import Experiment

ex = Experiment()

class A:
    @ex.config
    @classmethod
    def config(cls):
        """It would be nice to have access to `cls` inside of this ConfigScope"""
        classname = cls.__name__

    @classmethod
    def captured(cls, classname):
        """Some function to be captured."""
        print(classname)

# Do capturing here because in my case the classmethod can't be captured with a decorator.
A.captured = ex.capture(A.captured)

@ex.automain
def main():
    A.captured()
```

## Why this fails
### ConfigScope

The ``ConfigScope`` discards the context of the decorated function or method.
Because of this ``cls`` is not accessible from inside of the config scope. This
can be solved by saving the ``func.__self__`` attribute in 
``ConfigScope.__init__`` if ``func`` is an instance of ``types.MethodType``
and add this to ``preset`` as the first argument when calling the config scope.

### captured function

Capturing a classmethod already works if the function is captured _before_ 
transforming it into a classmethod:

```python
class A:
    @classmethod
    @ex.capture
    def captured(cls, ...):
      pass
```

However, this is not possible in my case. To make it work even _after_ 
converting the function into a classmethod I need to unwrap the function
and wrap it again into a classmethod (because a ``classmethod`` is immutable
  so setting additional attributes like logger and signature is not possible):

```python
class A:
    @classmethod
    def captured(cls, ...):
      pass

# This should work, but looks very ugly
A.captured = types.MethodType(ex.capture(A.captured.__func__), A)
```

This looks very ugly.
``Ingredient.capture`` could be modified to handle this unwrapping and wrapping
if the passed function is an instance of ``types.MethodType``.

I'd like to hear some thoughts about this and whether this could be included 
in sacred.

Btw, this should also work for instance methods.

issue_title: Remove @inject decorator
issue_body:
There is a feedback that ```@inject``` decorator is potential source of really annoying errors.

Documentation - http://python-dependency-injector.ets-labs.org/en/stable/advanced_usage/index.html#inject-decorator

Resolution: should be removed in first 3.x.x release.

issue_title: [Question] Flask and dependency_injector
issue_body:
First off; thank you for much for such a wonderful tool. I enjoy using this a lot. I have a question that I hope you can educate me on.

I am writing a client-service which can handle rest calls.
When I make a request, I want to do some i/o on the client machine folder (I write to a folder) so it feeds some internal process with data (proprietary legacy services) and I scan another folder for a response from said legacy service and I take action (make a rest call posting what was found).
I am using watchdog observer for the directory/folder scan.

**client-service:** I have a controller (blueprint) which delegates to a service and I made them threadSafeSingletons. I injected the observer into the service as a threadSafeSingleton which starts scanning for changes on service instantiation.

This is because observer runs on a separate thread and I want it to be long running, and any new instances scanning the same folder throws errors. Seeing as flask creates a new context on every request and assuming I run this using wsgi, is the threadsafe singleton going to be fine?
fyi: I have not tested this on some wsgi server yet. Thanks!

issue_title: -c parameter ignored if ~/.aws/credentials exists
issue_body: Berry always ignores the -c parameter if ~/.aws/credentials exists, even if a full path is given. If this directory if renamed, everything works fine.

issue_title: fp.isNegative does not handle NaN properly
issue_body:
This is on the unstable branch.

For this benchmark:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic QF_FP)
(declare-fun s0 () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(assert
   (let ((s1 (and (fp.isNaN s0) (fp.isNegative s0))))
   s1))
(check-sat)
(get-value (s0))
```

Z3 produces:

```
sat
((s0 (_ NaN 11 53)))
```

Which suggests `NaN` is accepted by the predicate `fp.isNegative`. According to the formalization, `fp.isNegative` holds for `-0.0` and when the number `lt` compares to `0` successfully, neither of which is true for `NaN`.
