issue_title: TransposedConv2DLayer: output_size argument
issue_body:
The `TransposedConv2DLayer` currently produces an output of the minimum size that could have produced its input in a forward convolution. Sometimes that's not what you want: For a encoder/decoder architecture, you'll want each transposed convolution in the decoder to recreate the input shape of the corresponding forward convolution in the encoder. For example, convolving a 32x32 input with 3x3 filters, stride 2x2 and "same" padding will produce a 16x16 output, but applying the corresponding transposed convolution will produce a 31x31 output only (because a 31x31 input would have resulted in a 16x16 output as well).

To solve this, as agreed in #610, we should add an optional `output_size` argument to the `TransposedConv2DLayer` constructor that allows to override the inferred minimum output size.

issue_title: Save state of voyager and polestar
Have objects returned by openbin inherit from `IO.IOBase`
issue_body:
To support interfacing with the Python standard library, "file-like" objects should extend the [`IO.IOBase`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/io.html#io.IOBase) class (not really an abstract base class, but does nothing by itself), and the `fs.test.FSTestCases.openbin` updated accordingly with a `self.assertIsInstance(...)`.

Following classes are concerned, since the other filesystems already return a proper child object (`OSFS` and derived, `ZipFS`, `TarFS`)
* `fs.ftpfs.FTPFile`
* `fs.iotools.RawWrapper`
* `fs.memoryfs._MemoryFile`

I can provide a PR if you don't have the time to work on this @willmcgugan.

NB: neither `_MemoryFile` nor `FTPFile` implement `seekable`, `writeable` or `readable`, so they should be implemented to avoid returning `False` all the time like in the default `ÌOBase` implementation.

issue_title: Spellcheck in markdown cells
issue_body:
This issue outlines a variety of thoughts on spellchecking in markdown cells in the notebook.

**Problem:** Spellchecking is currently unavailable in CodeMirror because as of CodeMirror version 4.0, CodeMirror elements are not recognized as editable content areas by the browser. As a result, the system spellchecker is not instantiated in those editable elements.

This problem is not unique to the Jupyter ecosystem and is also not unique to the CodeMirror editor.

**Solutions:**
- Implement a spellcheck extension using CodeMirror extensions and a spellcheck library like typo.js.
    - Pros: Can be used by other people in the CodeMirror ecosystem
    - Cons: Lots of maintenance overhead, hard to expand for multiple langagues, will change as CodeMirror API changes
- Use a separate editor for markdown cells
    - Pros: Don't have to worry about CodeMirror shennagins with spellcheck at all
    - Cons: Have to worry about more components being loaded on the page
        - This can be alleviated by rendering some components statically when they are not in use
    - Cons: Will make cross-cell interactions (like shifting cell focus) difficult as context has to be switched between two different types of components

I was initially leaning towards the first option, but really the second option seems more viable at this point. It limits the changes that we would have to make to our codebase only and helps us avoid having to build and maintain another library or tool (despite the fact that it is very much so needed in the CodeMirror ecosystem).

We can think about how to make the cell focus stuff play nicely with this as well since that has been a topic of thought.

issue_title: Fallback not working for StreamField
issue_body:
Hi folks,

When a `StreamField` is empty for a given translated language `wagtail-modeltranslation` doesn't fallback to the default language, as `django-modeltranslation` does for Django's standard fields.

I've traced the issue to `django-modeltranslation`'s `TranslationFieldDescriptor` [meaningful_value()](https://github.com/deschler/django-modeltranslation/blob/master/modeltranslation/fields.py#L324-L331) which, not surprisingly, doesn't account for `StreamField`'s particularities.

I'll submit a PR proposing a patch to address this.

When building ncclient with python-3.x in a C locale (i.e. not UTF-8), it fails with:

    return codecs.ascii_decode(input, self.errors)[0]

The problem is that one of the contributors listed in `README.rst` is using UTF-8 characters.

issue_title: No timeout on "connect"
issue_body:
It appears that there's no timeout for the "connect" function.  If you try to connect to a serial port that exists but is not actually connected to a vehicle, it just hangs.

On the RasPi, I'm conecting to the Pixhawk via USB, so it's /dev/ttyACM0. The RasPi has a built in serial port at ttyAMA0. If you "Connect('/dev/ttyAM0')" it hangs, presumably because it's a valid port so it thinks it can read, but never gets valid data.

issue_title: listIndex error out of range on 'normal' videos with bitrate based quality instead of HxW format
issue_body:
streams.py tries to split the quality returned by 'x'. This fails with exceptions if the quality is something like '128k' which is quite common for the 'normal' type videos that are mp4 based (they're rated by bitrate instead of aspect ratio/dimensions)

This breaks 'maxres' and getq() tests in streams.py for checking the max resolution and the width fetching for sorting.

This requires a different setting for max bitrate as well as max resolution. So you need to add a 'max bitrate' setting and test that value when a 'k' is found in the quality. For now I have patched my own copy of mpsyt with that idea.

issue_title: clean: make frame_slice_list private
planning v0.5.0 release
issue_body:
I think we have reached a sufficient number of new features that it would be nice to do another release soon. Does @PyWavelets/core agree? Any suggested date to target?

Are there other things people would like to see prior to release?  If so please add to the checklist below

**Since 0.4.0 we have merged:**
- 1D continuous wavelet transforms
- new discrete wavelets added (additional Debauchies and Coiflet wavelets)
- new 'reflect' extension mode for discrete wavelet transforms
- routines to convert multilevel DWT coefficients to and from single array
- substantial refactoring/reorganization of the underlying C and Cython code

**and could probably finish the existing PRs for:**
- faster performance for multilevel stationary wavelet transforms
- axis support for multilevel DWT
- (maybe?) benchmarking via ASV

TODO
- [x] add documentation for CWT
- [x] document new DWT coefficient reshaping routines (PR at #208)
- [x] update index.rst of the docs to reflect new features  (PR at #207)
- [x] update release notes (PR at #206)
- [x] handful of existing PRs to review/merge #179,  (#158?)
- [x] iswt and iswt2 from the last release should also be added to the API documentation
- [x] fix or remove broken doctests (see issue #209)

issue_title: Let gen_remote() require a URL
issue_body:
1. Describe what you expected to happen.

[`pulp_smash.tests.pulp3.file.api_v3.utils.gen_remote`](http://pulp-smash.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/pulp_smash.tests.pulp3.file.api_v3.utils.html#pulp_smash.tests.pulp3.file.api_v3.utils.gen_remote) returns a valid dict for use in creating a remote.

2. Describe what actually happened.

The returned dict is incomplete. A URL must be injected into the returned dict.

3. Describe what version of Pulp and what OS is affected by the issue.

Pulp 3.

4. State steps to re-create the issue.

Execute a call like the following:

```python
client.post(FILE_REMOTE_PATH, gen_remote())  # Boom!
```

This will fail. It would be better if `gen_remote` required a URL:

```python
client.post(FILE_REMOTE_PATH, gen_remote('valid url'))
```

In those cases where one wishes to send a malformed request, the returned dict can be saved:

```python
body = gen_remote('bogus url')
del body['url']
client.post(FILE_REMOTE_PATH, body)  # Boom!
```

5. If there is a related pulp.plan.io issue, link to it, and set the "smash test" field.

6. Choose proper labels. See: [Smash Labels](https://pulp-smash.readthedocs.io/en/latest/about.html#labels)

Done.

issue_title: `make lint` blows up on Python 3.5
issue_body: The `lint` make target calls `pylint`, which makes use of astroid. From my reading of the Travis log files, astroid does not yet work well on Python 3.5. We should skip this particular test for Python 3.5. For an example of how to conditionally execute a `script:` element in `.travis.yml`, see Tornado's `.travis.yml`: https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado/blob/master/.travis.yml

issue_title: Test re publish repository after un associating content
issue_body:
A repository need to re published to regenerate metadata after un associating content .This is to test that repository can be republished and the metadata is regenerated with no reference to the content removed.


1. Create a repository

pulp-admin rpm repo create --repo-id=foo --feed=https://repos.fedorapeople.org/repos/pulp/pulp/demo_repos/zoo/

2. Synchronize the repository
pulp-admin rpm repo sync run --repo-id=foo

3. List the rpm
pulp-admin rpm repo content rpm --repo-id foo

4. Remove an errata
pulp-admin -vv rpm repo remove cat --repo-id=foo --in='id=cat'

5. List the rpm
pulp-admin rpm repo content rpm --repo-id foo
Assert:
The rpm cat is not listed

6. Publish the repository

pulp-admin rpm repo publish run --repo-id foo
Assert:
Repository get published

7. Test that the "rpm" can not be accessed from the repo

Not sure which is the best way to test this one

The options are
a. Looking in the repodata to make sure that all reference cat is removed

b. Access the pulp repo through yum or pulp install.

issue_title: [A2C] The initial observation is wrong?
issue_body:
The initial observations in the current implementation are *zero vectors*, instead of the observation returned from the `env.reset()`. See [here](https://github.com/openai/baselines/blob/master/baselines/a2c/a2c.py#L90-L146)


[Line 100 in a2c/a2c.py](https://github.com/openai/baselines/blob/master/baselines/a2c/a2c.py#L100) should be modified to be:

```python
self.obs = env.reset()
```

issue_title: Requirements not clearly stated

The document doesn't state upfront that the code requires Python 3 to run; I only realized this when I got an error about `no module named builtins`.

I realise most people who install this will probably have that module anyway, but in cases where they don't, the earliest they'll realize something is wrong is when they try to execute the `python -m baselines.deepq.experiments.train_cartpole` example and fail.

issue_title: Tracing errors is hard
Mutations now have no access to context, resolve methods broken
issue_body:
The context story seems very confused in the newest version. My understanding from [the graphql-js 0.5.0 release notes](https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/releases/tag/v0.5.0) is that all resolving functions should now have the signature `([self,] args, context, info)`, as opposed to `([self,] args, info)`.

At the moment, if I update a `resolve_NAME` method to take context, then it throws argument errors.

Also, mutations don't take context and therefore they cannot access context data. It think a patch similar to [this one](https://github.com/graphql/graphql-relay-js/pull/77) on graphql-relay-js is necessary for the relay mutations at least, but probably for all of them.

issue_title: Escaped JSONStrings cause GraphQLSyntaxError
Dynamic ObjectType properties?
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm trying to get Graphene to return a GraphQL response for dynamically created SQL tables.

(The code I'm using so far is posted below)

Is there a way to dynamically specify the DatasourceType instead of having to hardcode it as a class?

Maybe an edge case but I thought I'd ask anyway.

Thanks, great library by the way!

Gijs


```python
import graphene
from django.db import connection
from collections import namedtuple


class DatasourceType(graphene.ObjectType):

    id = graphene.String()
    COLUMN_A = graphene.String()
    COLUMN_B = graphene.String()
    COLUMN_C = graphene.String()


class QueryType(graphene.ObjectType):

    class Meta:
        name = 'Query'

    datasources = graphene.List(DatasourceType)

    def resolve_datasources(a, b, c, d):
        return get_datasources()


def get_db_rows(sql, args=None):
    cursor = connection.cursor()
    cursor.execute(sql, args)
    columns = [col[0] for col in cursor.description]
    RowType = namedtuple('Row', columns)
    data = [
        RowType(*row)
        for row in cursor.fetchall()]
    cursor.close()
    return data


def get_datasources():
    return get_db_rows("""SELECT * FROM "someschema"."sometable"
    """)


schema = graphene.Schema(query=QueryType)
```

issue_title: Enum default_value serialization issue
issue_body:
Using graphene 2.0.1

```
class MyEnum(graphene.Enum):
    FOO=0
    BAR=1

foobar = graphene.List(FooBar, my_enum=graphene.Argument(MyEnum, default_value=MyEnum.FOO.name))
```

This results in the defaultValue being "\"FOO\"". This does not follow the graphql spec for enums and breaks with third party introspection. Specifically i'm trying to stitch a graphene remote schema and the invalid defaultValue results in a null returned in the merged schema (using graphql-tools).

```
class MyEnum(graphene.Enum):
    FOO="FOO"
    BAR="BAR"
```
This also doesn't work with using MyEnum.FOO.value for default_value nor setting it specifically to the string "FOO"

I tried looking for how default_value gets resolved for enums but haven't been able to track it down. I think if we can just get the string value for default_value to not include the escaped string quotes then my schema stitching will work. I haven't been able to figure out how to define an enum so that this will happen.

issue_title: Not able to request EmbeddedDocumentField in query
Slicing for last n nodes is broken
How to create a field to expose Fields' choices
Auto schema generation
issue_body:
So I started playing around with `graphene-django` last night. We have a complex data model so I don't want to write a shadow graphql schema for our models. We have a similar approach for generating DRF serializers using metaprogramming so I decided to try something with  and currently have a query that generates  a schema from our app:

```python 
from django.apps import apps
from graphene import relay, ObjectType, AbstractType, Schema, Node
from graphene_django import DjangoObjectType
from graphene_django.filter import DjangoFilterConnectionField


def build_query_objs():
    queries = {}
    models = apps.get_app_config('our_app_name').get_models()
    for model in models:
        model_name = model.__name__
        node = type('{model_name}Node'.format(model_name=model_name),
                    (DjangoObjectType,),
                    dict(
                        Meta=type('Meta',
                                  (object,),
                                  dict(model=model,
                                       interfaces=(relay.Node,))
                                  )
                    )
                    )
        queries.update({model_name: relay.Node.Field(node)})
        queries.update({'all_{model_name}'.format(model_name=model_name): DjangoFilterConnectionField(node)})
    return queries

Query = type('Query', (ObjectType,), build_query_objs())

schema = Schema(query=Query)
```

This worked well but noticed that I couldn't filter on any fields so I have more code (on dev machine atm) that generates `filter_fields`, and `order_filter_field` arguments for the generated `Meta` classes. With a single pass I can filter on 1 thing. I'd like to be able to filter on any nested filters eg `customer__order__note_contains` or something. Is there a good to get all these query-filter strings for each model?

It would be awesome if you left the `filter_fields` blank that all possible filters would be generated for the node Meta class.

issue_title: api: simplify OT api by removing any locking/concurrency support.
issue_body:
Currently, a subset of OT calls are designed to be called from interrupt context when another call to OT may be interrupted.  It is preferable for all locking/concurrency handling to be handled outside of OT, as this is necessarily platform-specific.  This would also greatly simplify the `otPlat*` driver APIs.

Remove any locking/concurrency handling from OT would mean:

1. Removing the Atomic platform abstraction and any use of Atomic within OT.
2. Removing all `otPlat*Signal*` calls into OT.
3. Updating the POSIX example to use a single thread of execution.

issue_title: FR: Openthread Platform Abstraction for 802.15.4 MLME-SAP and MCPS-SAP
issue_body:
Hi, I am trying to investigate the possibility of running openthread on top of an external 802.15.4 MAC layer, using the 802.15.4 defined MLME-SAP and MCPS-SAP.

So far, I have successfully got a mostly-working port by:
-Modifying Mac.cpp to use SAP primitives rather than raw PHY frames and manage the PiB security tables as necessary
-Completely changing radio.h to provide a SAP-primitive interface
-Modify MeshForwarder.cpp to use primitives instead of directly building frames, and send indirect transmissions direct to the MAC to queue in the indirect queue
-A few very small modifications dotted around other parts of the stack to allow for getting/giving necessary information for/from the external PIB

I have 2 main questions

1. Would a modification like this have the potential to be merged into the main openthread branch as an optional module? I see a few advantages:
    1. Better platform compatibility by providing an alternate adaptation point
    2. Delegate all of the tougher real-time requirements of 802.15.4 to a coprocessor/real time module which is responsible for the 802.15.4 MAC
    3. Isolate the security processing at the 802.15.4 layer from the rest of openthread

    and I see a few different potential ways of implementation:

    1. As I have it structured currently: a completely different path from meshForwarder down, configured using compile time options to compile different files
    2. Restructure the stack to use SAP primitives entirely and have an optional Mac class which can run below this for interacting with a PHY radio

    Personally, I see i as the more realistic option, although it would result in a less unified stack. Option ii would probably result in more resource usage than openthread's current model for the PHY configuration.

2. I have some questions about the usage of Key Id mode 2 at the MAC layer, specifically the usage of the 'Mode 2 Extended Address'. The Thread spec seems to indicate (and openthread currently seems to match) that when a message is sent with mode2, the originating address should be aExtendedAddress, but the address used in the nonce for CCM should be 0x3506feb823d48712. This seems to be unachievable with a plain 802.15.4 MAC, as section 7.6.3.2 in the IEEE802.15.4 spec states that in the construction of the nonce "The source address shall be set to the extended address aExtendedAddress of the device originating the frame". I also note that section 3.2 of the thread spec states that 7.6.3.2 of the IEEE802.15.4 spec is normative.

    The rest of the thread spec seems to very naturally fit with a MAC-level implementation. And even the rest of the mode2 description seems to fit with the 802.15.4 spec, such as structuring the entire thing around the device descriptor (including forcing a frame counter reset every received frame so it can be used globally).

    So:

    1. Which is correct, using 0x3506feb823d48712 as aExtendedAddress temporarily, or injecting it into the nonce outside of the 802.15.4 spec's guidelines?
    2. I know the mode2 is supposed to aid in 'filtering', but seems to cause a great deal of extra processing, how is it supposed to work?

Apologies for the long issue, I would appreciate any help or comments,

Thanks in advance,
Ciaran Woodward

issue_title: Problem: installation guide for devs has too much information
issue_body:
Solution: restructure the installation guide and make it more usable

The [Installation Guide for Developers](https://docs.bigchaindb.com/projects/py-driver/en/latest/quickstart.html#installation-guide-for-developers) needs to be updated and improved.
First of all it is not only an installation guide which is misleading. It is actually a guide to interact with github. 
The guide can be merged with [Installing from the Source Code](https://docs.bigchaindb.com/projects/py-driver/en/latest/advanced-installation.html).
The guide needs to have a new name and needs to be restructured. It's fine to explain the whole process to get the repository and how to commit+push it when changes were made. But additional to that we need to show how to set up the dev environment properly.

To Do:
- [ ] Don't link to `Advanced Installation Options` just move it to the section (this should not just be advanced, but also the guide for devs, but only for installation(!)
- [ ] Instead of cloning we should tell developers to fork, linking to the GitHub forking example should be good enough
- [ ] Remove all steps after a successful installation and link to the Contributing section instead
- [ ] Update [Installing the Driver](https://docs.bigchaindb.com/projects/py-driver/en/latest/quickstart.html#installing-the-driver) to include the way to install alpha, beta, etc: [example](https://docs.bigchaindb.com/projects/py-driver/en/master/readme.html#install)


Open for comments/discussion/better ideas

issue_body:
I'm using `certbot` on Ubuntu 16.04, installed from `ppa:certbot/certbot`. My server is failing to renew the certificates; I have a suspicion that this has to do with where the HTTP daemon is looking for the `/.well-known/acme-challenge/` directory and am attempting to debug. I found the `--debug-challenge` flag, and tried to use it:
```
$ sudo certbot renew --no-self-upgrade --cert-name linestarve.com --debug-challenge
Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Processing /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/linestarve.com.conf
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Cert is due for renewal, auto-renewing...
Renewing an existing certificate
Performing the following challenges:
http-01 challenge for linestarve.com
Waiting for verification...

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Challenges loaded. Press continue to submit to CA. Pass "-v" for more info about
challenges.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```
This looks promising, and I began to debug the problem. Unfortunately, a few seconds later it forged ahead, heedless of my attempts to halt it:
```
Cleaning up challenges
Attempting to renew cert from /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/linestarve.com.conf produced an unexpected error: Failed authorization procedure. linestarve.com (http-01): urn:acme:error:unauthorized :: The client lacks sufficient authorization :: Invalid response from http://linestarve.com/.well-known/acme-challenge/PkEEMAWYYIhIfo8awvYBOKXZqnkIOlDruIYUgBDttEk:

Some investigating indicates that the `notify(..., pause=True)` argument is ignored in noninteractive mode (which `renew` runs in), so I tried `--force-interactive`. Unfortunately that results in "`--force-interactive cannot be used with renew`".

Is there a way to use `--debug-challenges` while renewing a certificate?

issue_title: Where to explain performance tips ?
issue_body:
Hello,
in our struggle for performance we've found a cheap optimization : using the C++ version of protobuf instead of the python one. It's all decribed [here](https://github.com/google/protobuf/tree/v2.6.1/python) : just set two environment variables before you run your program (PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION=cpp and ROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION_VERSION=2) and you get speedup for free (25 % in our usecase without quantization nor winding order). You might ask why version 2.6.1 ? Because we're on debian 8, and we use the same python package version than the apt python-protobuf package.

So, now my question is : is there somewhere in the project where we can leave track of this tip ?

issue_title: Return fixed-precision record data
issue_body:
The salesforce CurrencyType object has fields which are fixed-precision, but simple_salesforce returns a `float`. This is very likely to cause rounding errors since the intended use of the currency conversion rate is to multiply it.

For example

```
>>> salesforce.query('SELECT ConversionRate, DecimalPlaces, IsActive, IsCorporate, IsoCode FROM CurrencyType')
[OrderedDict([(u'attributes', OrderedDict([(u'type', u'CurrencyType'), (u'url', u'/services/data/v29.0/sobjects/CurrencyType/01L3000000ABCDEF')])), (u'ConversionRate', 0.63), (u'DecimalPlaces', 2), (u'IsActive', True), (u'IsCorporate', False), (u'IsoCode', u'GBP')]),
 OrderedDict([(u'attributes', OrderedDict([(u'type', u'CurrencyType'), (u'url', u'/services/data/v29.0/sobjects/CurrencyType/01L3000000ABCDEG')])), (u'ConversionRate', 1.0),  (u'DecimalPlaces', 2), (u'IsActive', True), (u'IsCorporate', True),  (u'IsoCode', u'USD')])]
```

Note the `(u'ConversionRate', 1.0)` value.

Looking at the code, you have `return result.json(object_pairs_hook=OrderedDict)`

That `json` function also exposes a `parse_float` parameter which says 

>     ``parse_float``, if specified, will be called with the string
    of every JSON float to be decoded. By default this is equivalent to
    float(num_str). This can be used to use another datatype or parser
    for JSON floats (e.g. decimal.Decimal).

Currently I'm overriding `query` so that can pass in `Decimal` as the docstring suggests. It would be good to have a way to get this behavior either by default or by configuration.

Here's some potential interfaces you might use if you want to make this configurable rather than the default.

```
sf = Salesforce(username=..., ..., parse_float=decimal.Decimal)
# or
sf.query(q, parse_float=decimal.Decimal)
# or
sf.set_float_parser(decimal.Decimal)
```

issue_title: Propagate alarm clock task exceptions
issue_body:
All exceptions that happen inside alarm clock tasks are ignored and only their stack trace is printed. This can lead to bugs being missed and should be avoided.

Any exception inside the alarm clock tasks **is a bug**. So we should [propagate](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/tasks.py#L101) all exceptions from the alarm tasks to the main task and let the exception be raised and quit the program.

issue_title: Remove block_number from the events storage
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The block number is provided through the
RaidenService.handle_state_change function, the block number used in
this function varies with the type of the state change.

Events from the blockchain can use a consistent value (the block number
from the event), but state changes for user actions or protocol messages
must use the current block number known by the node, which will vary on
restart. To avoid inconsistencies on the block_number on restarts, this
value should be removed.

WIP at #1859

issue_title: Fix generation of package via setup.py
Exception on delete of polymorphic collection
issue_body:
```python
class Order(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(_("Title"), max_length=200)

    class Meta:
        ordering = ('title',)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title

class Payment(PolymorphicModel):
    order = models.ForeignKey(Order, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    amount = models.DecimalField(default=0, blank=True, max_digits=10, decimal_places=2)
    index = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0, blank=False)

    class Meta:
        ordering = ('index',)

class CreditCardPayment(Payment):
    card_type = models.CharField()

class Beneficiary(models.Model):
    firstname = models.CharField()
    lastname = models.CharField()

class SepaPayment(Payment):
    iban = models.CharField()
    bic = models.CharField()
    beneficiaries = models.ManyToManyField(Beneficiary, "sepa", blank=True)
```

When deleting an Order whose first `Payment` is of type `SepaPayment`, an exception is raised :
`Exception Value: Cannot query <CreditCardPayment>: Must be "SepaPayment" instance.`

This is because of this code in django core: https://github.com/django/django/blob/master/django/db/models/deletion.py#L193

The model type is taken from the first element in the polymorphic collection

Minimal example here: https://github.com/unusual-thoughts/django-poly-bug

issue_title: Potential problems with the reuse of .task files
issue_body:
We have `.def`, `.task`, and `.res` dedicated to task definition (no host-specific information), tasks with host information, and results of tasks. This allowed us to generate multiple tasks files for a single `.def` file and multiple `.res` files for the same task (executed on different remote hosts).

With the consolidation of all these files into a single task file (#991), we can still do these but not concurrently. For example,

```
sos execute task -q hostA &
sos execute task -q hostB 
```
will fail because both sos instances will write to the same `.task` file. After the completion of the tasks, the results will be passed back embedded in the `.task` file so it is no longer possible to have results from different remote hosts.

These are rare use scenarios but we should perhaps do something about them later.

issue_title: Embracing asyncio / Python >= 3.5?

issue_title: Add persistent key-value storage
issue_body:
It must be global - all keys accessible from all checks.
It must persist between restarts (saved on disk).
Writes should be made in notify:
```yaml
checks:
  - name: Add to stash
    script: echo "Good"
    notify:
      - stash:
          Hubert: {{ content }} news, everyone! 
```
stash notifier accepts dictionary, where each value is a Jinja template.
All keys are written atomically, e.g. if an error happens in one, none will be written.
Stash should be accessible wherever conf is (fetchers, transforms, notifiers) and should be named `stash`.

It will unlock checks aggregation.

issue_title: Closing a stream doesn't affect readers
issue_body:
If a stream is read from in task A and closed in task B, one would assume that the `await receive_some()` called by A reads an EOF, raises an exception, gets cancelled, or whatever.

Instead, it just sits there, doing nothing.

The underlying reason is that closed file descriptors are silently removed from `epoll`. Apparently, that is regarded as a feature. As certain other asyncio libraries are able to handle this situation, we need to at least document that.

issue_title: UnLock should be refactored
issue_body:
* The name is too clever by half. Or at least too clever by, say, 5%.

* It should probably be refactored into a dedicated tool for raising `ResourceBusyError`, since that's how every caller uses it.

issue_title: Not sure we're propagating cancellation from fixtures in the most ideal way
issue_body:
As noted here: https://github.com/python-trio/pytest-trio/pull/50#issuecomment-413817328

> In the course of this (and also while thinking about python-trio/trio#607), I have started to think that maybe the details of our new cancellation rules aren't quite right: I'm leaning towards saying that when a fixture crashes, we should cancel all "downstream" fixtures, but not do anything at all to independent fixtures. Right now, we only cancel the test, not other downstream fixtures... except that we also cancel all fixtures that haven't started yet, including independent ones. This means that if you have two independent fixtures that both crash during setup, you'll actually get different exceptions on different runs, randomly, depending on which one happens to run first.

issue_title: Request to add whitelisted domains to HCA Documentation Page
issue_body: On the HCA Documentation page, a user has to click on a link to open a file with the list of whitelisted domains. We should include the whitelisted domains to the HCA docs http://hca.readthedocs.io/en/latest/  removing the need to click on the link.

issue_title: Wait for all tasks to finish before shutting down the DFK
issue_body: We should not shut down the DFK until all tasks have finished. We could add a `wait` or `join` method which the user could call to wait until pending tasks are completed, although I am interested if someone could describe a use case for calling such a function anywhere except the end of the script, in a system where dependencies are taken care of automatically. I favor assuming that the user definitely wants all of their tasks to finish, so we automatically call such a function at the end of the script, and do not require the user to do so.

issue_title: Exception handling
issue_body:
Exception handling in Parsl should support the following cases:

1. Exceptions raised or exit codes returned
2. File outputs specified but missing
3. Input futures failed
4. Walltime exceeded
5. [Later] Data staging failed

We should also support this same set for both Bash and Python apps.

Unknowns:
1. Where in the code should an exception be raised/handled for asynchronous apps.
2. Retries and retry condition checks.

issue_title: Stats: Avoid calling .items() on outer class
issue_body: For example `TagMap` has a property `_map` which is a dictionary. We should call `tag_map._map.items()` instead of calling `tag_map.items()`: https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/stats/view_data.py#L77.

issue_title: Error ONNX Export failed, Hardtanh not supported
issue_body:
I tried to export a PyTorch model to ONNX and got this warning and error : 

`UserWarning: ONNX export failed on ATen operator hardtanh because torch.onnx.symbolic.hardtanh does not exist`
`RuntimeError: ONNX export failed: Couldn't export operator aten::hardtanh`

So after some researches, I understood that PyTorch can't export the operator Hardtanh. I tried to follow [the tutorial to add export support ](https://github.com/onnx/tutorials/blob/master/tutorials/PytorchAddExportSupport.md) but it seems like Hardtanh is not a standardized operator in ONNX. For this case, the tutorial says I have to add it to ONNX. How can I do that ? Is it possible to achieve this or should I wait for this operator to be supported or find another way to convert my model ?
